description: Add missing Storybook stories for side panel components: ErrorBoundary, PluginList, OutdatedPluginsBadge, and ConfirmationDialog

userStories.title: Add ErrorBoundary storybook story
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Storybook story for the ErrorBoundary component so that I can visually verify the error fallback UI in isolation
acceptanceCriteria: Story renders the error fallback state (the Alert with 'Something went wrong' message and Reload button)
Story renders the normal pass-through state (children rendered without error)
Story follows existing conventions: Meta with title 'Components/ErrorBoundary', w-80 decorator, named exports
userStories.notes: Component is at platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx. It's a class component with two states: (1) hasError=false renders children, (2) hasError=true renders Alert with 'Something went wrong' and a Reload button. To trigger the error state in a story, create a small helper component that throws during render (e.g., `const Thrower = () => { throw new Error('test'); }`) and wrap it in ErrorBoundary. For the happy path, just wrap normal content. Follow the pattern from FailedPluginCard.stories.tsx for simple stories. Storybook config is at platform/browser-extension/.storybook/main.ts — stories glob is '../src/**/*.stories.tsx' so the file will be auto-discovered. Use .ts extension imports in story (not .js) — existing stories use bare module imports without extensions.

userStories.title: Add PluginList storybook story
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Storybook story for the PluginList component so that I can visually verify plugin list rendering, failed plugin cards, tool filtering, and empty filter results
acceptanceCriteria: Story covers: default state with multiple plugins, state with failed plugins, tool filter matching, tool filter with no matches (empty state)
Story follows existing conventions: Meta with title 'Components/PluginList', w-80 decorator, named exports
Mock data follows the pattern from PluginCard.stories.tsx (mockPlugin helper with PluginState type)
userStories.notes: Component is at platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginList.tsx. Props: plugins (PluginState[]), failedPlugins (FailedPluginState[]), activeTools (Set<string>), setPlugins (Dispatch<SetStateAction<PluginState[]>>), toolFilter (string). Types PluginState and FailedPluginState are from '../bridge'. PluginState fields: name, displayName, version, trustTier, source, tabState, urlPatterns, sdkVersion, tools (array of {name, displayName, description, icon, enabled}). FailedPluginState fields: specifier, error. Reference PluginCard.stories.tsx (line 7-22) for the mockPlugin helper pattern. Use useState for setPlugins like PluginCard.stories.tsx does. Stories to create: (1) Default — 2-3 plugins, no filter; (2) WithFailedPlugins — mix of healthy and failed plugins; (3) FilteredByTool — toolFilter='send' showing matching results; (4) NoFilterMatch — toolFilter='nonexistent' showing empty state message. The component renders FailedPluginCard items followed by an Accordion of PluginCard items.

userStories.title: Add OutdatedPluginsBadge storybook story
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Storybook story for the OutdatedPluginsBadge component so that I can visually verify the badge, dropdown, and copy button behavior
acceptanceCriteria: Story covers: single outdated plugin, multiple outdated plugins, empty list (renders nothing)
Story follows existing conventions: Meta with title 'Components/OutdatedPluginsBadge', w-80 decorator, named exports
userStories.notes: Component is at platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/OutdatedPluginsBadge.tsx. Props: outdatedPlugins (OutdatedPlugin[]). OutdatedPlugin interface: { name: string, currentVersion: string, latestVersion: string, updateCommand: string }. The component renders nothing when outdatedPlugins is empty. When non-empty, shows a red badge with count and chevron. Clicking opens a dropdown with plugin name, version range (current → latest), and a copy button for the update command. CopyButton is an internal component that uses navigator.clipboard. Stories to create: (1) SinglePlugin — one outdated plugin; (2) MultiplePlugins — 3 outdated plugins to show scrolling; (3) Empty — empty array, should render null. The badge is absolutely positioned (dropdown), so the decorator should have some padding-top to show the dropdown below.

userStories.title: Add ConfirmationDialog storybook story
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a Storybook story for the ConfirmationDialog component so that I can visually verify the approval dialog, countdown bar, scope dropdown, and multi-confirmation navigation
acceptanceCriteria: Story covers: single confirmation, single confirmation with domain, single confirmation with params preview, multiple confirmations with navigation
CountdownBar animates correctly (uses receivedAt timestamp relative to Date.now())
Story follows existing conventions: Meta with title 'Components/ConfirmationDialog', w-80 decorator, named exports
userStories.notes: Component is at platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ConfirmationDialog.tsx. Props: confirmations (ConfirmationData[]), onRespond (id, decision, scope?) callback, onDenyAll () callback. ConfirmationData is SpConfirmationRequestMessage['data'] & { receivedAt: number }. The SpConfirmationRequestMessage data fields: id (string), tool (string), domain (string | null), tabId? (number), paramsPreview (string), timeoutMs (number). The receivedAt field must be set to Date.now() in the story so the CountdownBar animates from full to zero. The component imports from '../../extension-messages.js' — the story can construct mock data inline without importing that type (just satisfy the shape). Stories to create: (1) SingleConfirmation — one confirmation with tool name and domain, timeoutMs=30000, receivedAt=Date.now(); (2) WithParamsPreview — includes paramsPreview JSON string; (3) NoDomain — domain is null, so the AllowAlwaysButton dropdown should not show 'For all tools on {domain}'; (4) MultipleConfirmations — 3 confirmations showing 'X of Y' counter and prev/next navigation. Use action() from @storybook/addon-actions for onRespond and onDenyAll callbacks if available, or use console.log wrappers.
